name: Lemon Custard
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Fill half of a 13 x 9-inch baking dish with water and place it in the oven.
3. Grease an 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2 inch loaf pan lightly.
4. In a large bowl, whisk together milk, egg substitute, sugar, and lemon peel until well blended. Pour into the prepared loaf pan.
5. Carefully place the loaf pan in the middle of the baking dish with water in the oven.
6. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes to 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove the loaf pan from the oven and place it on a wire rack. Allow to cool completely, then cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
7. For the Lemon Syrup, bring sugar and water to a rolling boil in a small saucepan, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Remove from heat and stir in lemon peel and juice. Cover and refrigerate.
To serve:
1. Run a thin sharp knife around the edge of the custard.
2. Invert serving plate over the pan.
3. Gently invert the plate and pan together, shaking them slightly from side to side to release the custard.
4. Spoon some syrup over the custard and serve the rest on the side. Garnish with raspberries and a lemon slice.
